comments: This ve(3,3) model really flips the script on traditional AMMs! Love how it puts power back in the hands of actual liquidity providers instead of VCs.
The bribe mechanism is genius - projects compete for liquidity while rewards flow back to the community. MarbMarket seems like exactly what DeFi needs.
No presale and no VC backing? Finally a fair launch where early supporters actually get rewarded for their conviction!
content:
Most people chase the latest memecoin pump. The real shift is happening where power returns to liquidity providers—not VCs who got tokens at $0.001.

MarbMarket is building the first veDEX on MegaETH. No presale. No VC backing. Just a fair launch where your MARB locks actually matter.

Here's how it works: you lock MARB → get veTokens → weekly votes decide which LP pools get emissions. Projects pay bribes for those votes → rewards flow back to you.

This is the ve(3,3) model in action. Longer locks = more influence + more rewards. The community decides where value flows, not some centralized team.

MegaETH needed this. A DEX where liquidity providers hold power, not just yield chasers. MarbMarket delivers that.

Check it out before launch: x.com/Marb_market

@RallyOnChain what's your take on ve(3,3) models replacing traditional AMMs?
